title: Elevator Failure/Interruption (Including Trapped People)

plan_procedures.action: Announce Code Brown and inform the Planning & Preparedness Unit through extension (3432).
Inform the Safety & Security Manager through extension (7777).

plan_procedures.responsible: Engineer on duty / Safety & Security Officer
plan_procedures.action: If there are trapped people: record names/time/location (elevator number/code); inquire number of trapped people and health status (elderly/infants/patients); provide support/instructions and reassurance until help arrives.
Dispatch technicians to inspect, determine the cause, and prioritize releasing trapped people.
Call the Rapid Response Team (RRT) when required.
Call the engineer in charge to conduct follow-up inspection and detect the cause of the issue.
If elevator maintenance is ongoing, switch off the elevator, label it as 'out of service' and under maintenance on each floor to prevent use until maintenance is completed.
After the issue is over, report and record findings and inform the Safety & Security Manager that the system returned to operation.
Inform DCCT that the failure/interruption ended and the system is back to work.
If Civil Defense is involved, obtain a copy of the report including remarks and authority.

learning_objectives: Manage elevator interruption and trapped-person response with correct documentation and escalation.
Ensure safety controls (out-of-service labeling) and complete reporting/coordination.

policy_chunks.title: Step 1
policy_chunks.text: Announce Code Brown and inform the Planning & Preparedness Unit through extension (3432).

policy_chunks.title: Step 2
policy_chunks.text: Inform the Safety & Security Manager through extension (7777).

policy_chunks.title: Step 3
policy_chunks.text: If there are trapped people: record names/time/location (elevator number/code); inquire number of trapped people and health status (elderly/infants/patients); provide support/instructions and reassurance until help arrives.

policy_chunks.title: Step 4
policy_chunks.text: Dispatch technicians to inspect, determine the cause, and prioritize releasing trapped people.

policy_chunks.title: Step 5
policy_chunks.text: Call the Rapid Response Team (RRT) when required.

policy_chunks.title: Step 6
policy_chunks.text: Call the engineer in charge to conduct follow-up inspection and detect the cause of the issue.

policy_chunks.title: Step 7
policy_chunks.text: If elevator maintenance is ongoing, switch off the elevator, label it as 'out of service' and under maintenance on each floor to prevent use until maintenance is completed.

policy_chunks.title: Step 8
policy_chunks.text: After the issue is over, report and record findings and inform the Safety & Security Manager that the system returned to operation.

policy_chunks.title: Step 9
policy_chunks.text: Inform DCCT that the failure/interruption ended and the system is back to work.

policy_chunks.title: Step 10
policy_chunks.text: If Civil Defense is involved, obtain a copy of the report including remarks and authority.
